message:
test(runtime): pin handle_native_event PaneDestroyed → PaneDisappeared emission (ft-pp7jk)

Regression test paired with 1f3ae436 (fix(runtime): publish
Event::PaneDisappeared from handle_native_event). That commit closed
the lifecycle contract — consumers already matching on
`Event::PaneDisappeared` in event_stream filters, wire_protocol
serializer, and main.rs handler were receiving nothing because the
production path never emitted it — but landed without a direct test.

This test feeds a `NativeEvent::PaneDestroyed` into
`handle_native_event` with an attached `EventBus`, then asserts a
subscriber receives `Event::PaneDisappeared { pane_id }` with the
matching pane id. A future regression that drops the `bus.publish(…)`
call (for example, under a refactor that threads the bus through a
different accessor) fails here loudly rather than silently leaking
subscribers back into the dormant state ft-pp7jk exists to prevent.

Also covers the None-bus path: when `event_bus` is absent, the
PaneDestroyed handler must be a no-op from an events perspective
(backpressure/tracker cleanup still runs; that's covered by the
existing `backpressure_cleanup_runs_on_pane_destroyed` test).

Test harness details:

- Builds a StorageHandle against a temp SQLite DB so the handler's
  storage path is exercised with real state.
- Pre-populates `cursors` and `detection_contexts` maps with a row
  for pane 42 so the destroy path exercises the cleanup branches
  on real keys rather than empty maps.
- Uses an in-process `crate::events::EventBus::new(16)` with a 16-
  slot ring; the publish happens synchronously before the subscriber
  read so no sleep/tokio-time is required.

No production code changes in this commit; test-only addition.
